title: AI frameworks
intro: High-level abstractions for building context-aware AI systems by composing models, prompts, tools, and control logic.

items.name: LangChain
items.description: General-purpose framework for building LLM-powered applications by composing prompts, models, and tools.
features: Abstractions for chains, agents, tools, and memory.
Broad ecosystem of integrations with models and vector stores.
Strong support for iterative development and experimentation.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use; commercial services available via LangSmith.

items.name: LlamaIndex
items.description: Data-centric framework focused on ingesting, structuring, and selecting context for LLM applications.
features: Indexing and retrieval abstractions for diverse data sources.
Flexible context assembly and query pipelines.
Emphasis on grounding model outputs in data.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use; paid cloud and enterprise offerings available.

items.name: Haystack
items.description: Open-source framework for building search-, retrieval-, and question-answering-centric AI systems.
features: Modular pipelines for retrieval, ranking, and generation.
Strong focus on production-ready RAG architectures.
Integration with multiple vector stores and model providers.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use; commercial offerings available through Deepset.

items.name: Spring AI
items.description: Enterprise-oriented framework for integrating LLMs and AI capabilities into Spring-based Java applications.
features: Familiar Spring abstractions for prompts, models, and embeddings.
Integration with enterprise systems and application infrastructure.
Emphasis on configuration, testability, and maintainability.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use.

items.name: DSPy
items.description: Declarative framework for building LLM applications using modular Python programs, replacing manual prompt engineering with structured pipelines.
features: Programming model based on signatures and modules instead of raw prompts.
Automatic optimization of prompts and model behavior.
Supports building RAG systems and agent workflows.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use.

items.name: AI SDK
items.description: Provider-agnostic TypeScript toolkit for building AI-powered applications and agents.
features: Unified API to interact with multiple model providers.
Native support for streaming, tool calling, and structured outputs.
Integration with modern web frameworks such as Next.js.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use.

items.name: Pydantic AI
items.description: Python framework for building production-grade AI applications and agents using typed models and validation based on Pydantic.
features: Strongly-typed interfaces for inputs and outputs using Pydantic models.
Model-agnostic architecture supporting multiple LLM providers.
Designed for building reliable and production-ready AI workflows.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use.

items.name: Agent Development Kit
items.description: Agent-focused development kit for building and operating AI agents within the Google ecosystem.
features: Native integration with Google models and cloud services.
Tool use, planning, and agent lifecycle management.
Emphasis on production deployment and platform integration.
items.pricing: Pricing depends on underlying Google Cloud services and model usage.

items.name: Microsoft Agent Framework
items.description: Open-source framework for building modular, agent-driven AI systems. Microsoft maintains this tool as an evolution of AutoGen.
features: Modular agent abstractions enabling reusable behaviors.
Integration patterns for tools, models, and memory components.
Emphasis on structured and interpretable agent workflows.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use.

items.name: CrewAI
items.description: Framework for orchestrating collaborative, role-based AI agents working toward shared goals.
features: Explicit roles, goals, and task delegation between agents.
Emphasis on coordination and collaboration patterns.
Designed for building agent teams with clear responsibilities.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use; paid plans for hosted and enterprise features.

items.name: Agno
items.description: Open-source framework and runtime for building and managing multi-agent AI systems with memory and tools.
features: Support for building agents and agentic workflows with shared memory.
Built-in components for tool integration and knowledge retrieval.
Includes a production runtime and control plane for managing agents.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use; enterprise features available via hosted offerings.

items.name: Parlant
items.description: Open-source conversational AI framework focused on building structured and controllable dialogue systems.
features: Declarative conversation design with explicit state and transitions.
Fine-grained control over dialogue flow and agent behavior.
Designed for reliable and production-grade conversational systems.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use.

items.name: DeepAgents
items.description: Open-source agent harness built on LangChain and LangGraph for creating structured, multi-step AI agents.
features: Built-in planning, task tracking, and sub-agent delegation.
Filesystem-backed context management to persist intermediate results.
Middleware-based architecture with capabilities such as summarization.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use.

items.name: OpenClaw
items.description: Open-source autonomous AI agent platform that runs locally and connects language models to external tools.
features: Local-first execution with integrations across messaging platforms.
Autonomous task execution, including web browsing and code execution.
Extensible skills system allowing agents to acquire new capabilities.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use.

items.name: Dify
items.description: Open-source platform for building and operating LLM applications, combining prompt engineering, RAG, agents, and workflows.
features: Unified interface for prompt design, workflows, and model integration.
Built-in RAG pipelines and dataset management.
Integrated observability and governance features.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use for self-hosting; managed cloud offering available.

items.name: LangFlow
items.description: Visual development environment for building LLM applications, RAG pipelines, and agents using a drag-and-drop interface.
features: Graph-based visual editor for composing prompts, models, tools, and data sources.
Integration with the Python and LLM application ecosystem.
Rapid prototyping of RAG pipelines and agent workflows.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use.

items.name: Flowise
items.description: Open-source visual development platform for building agents and LLM workflows.
features: Visual builders for chatflows, agents, and tool-using workflows.
API deployment, tracing, analytics, evaluations, and team workspaces.
Support for human-in-the-loop and production-oriented workflows.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use for self-hosting; managed cloud offering available.

items.name: Zapier
items.description: No-code automation platform that connects AI-driven actions with SaaS applications and business workflows.
features: Trigger-action workflows integrating AI with business tools.
Large ecosystem of prebuilt connectors.
Rapid deployment without custom infrastructure.
items.pricing: Free tier with limited automations; paid plans based on task volume and features.

items.name: n8n
items.description: Open-source workflow automation platform enabling visual orchestration of AI components and external systems.
features: Visual, node-based workflow editor.
Self-hosted or managed deployment options.
Control over data flow and execution logic.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use for self-hosting; paid plans for managed offerings.

items.name: Temporal
items.description: Durable execution platform for reliable long-running workflows used in production AI and agentic systems.
features: Fault-tolerant and stateful workflow execution.
Handling of retries, timeouts, failures, and external events.
Language-native workflow definitions for production software systems.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use; paid cloud and enterprise plans available.
